signals.idea: Video editor final polish round: (1) When timeline is locked/expanded on editor page, shrink video preview to give timeline more space, revert when unlocked; (2) Fix render - it worked in announcement-video, figure out what's missing; (3) Timeline clip dragging has no visual feedback showing where clip is being dragged to; (4) Text overlay dragging is sticky - container moves but text doesn't follow; (5) Image overlay dragging is sticky/inconsistent; (6) Condense timeline controls - move clear-unused, new-track buttons, zoom into the empty row space; (7) Clear X unused shouldn't count default V1/A1 tracks; (8) EditorPane should overlay content like GenerationsPane, not push it down; (9) Playhead in compact EditorPane not clickable

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 13 diagnosis is likely wrong — EditorPaneTab already returns null when on the full editor route, so duplicate VideoEditorProviders cannot coexist. The duplicate query warning likely has a different root cause (e.g., React strict mode double-mount, or two components sharing the same query key for different reasons).

Step 5 proposes calling `onOverlayChange` on every mouse move, but this triggers the full pipeline: applyTimelineEdit → materializeData → rowsToConfig → React state update → Remotion re-render. This could cause significant lag during drag, especially with complex timelines.

Step 9 render approach (MediaRecorder + canvas.captureStream) is speculative — Remotion Player does not render to a raw `` element; it uses a standard DOM tree with `
`, `

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 5.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

items: Compact preview playhead slider is clickable and draggable without crashing
No Remotion license warning appears in browser console
'Clear N unused' button does not count V1 and A1 in the unused count
Editor pane overlays page content instead of pushing it down with paddingTop
Text and image overlays visually follow the cursor during drag (not just on drop)
Timeline controls (add track, clear unused, zoom) are condensed into less vertical space
Preview area shrinks when timeline is maximized/expanded, reverts when restored
Render button produces a downloadable video file
Timeline clip dragging shows visual position feedback (ghost/indicator)
External file drag-drop onto timeline uploads to Supabase and creates a clip
Assets panel in PropertiesPanel starts collapsed by default
No duplicate React Query warnings in console
npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 13 diagnosis is likely wrong — EditorPaneTab already returns null when on the full editor route, so duplicate VideoEditorProviders cannot coexist. The duplicate query warning likely has a different root cause (e.g., React strict mode double-mount, or two components sharing the same query key for different reasons).
unresolved_flags.evidence: EditorPaneTab.tsx lines 67-69: `if (isVideoEditorRoute(location.pathname)) { return null; }` — the compact pane unmounts entirely when the full editor is active. The plan's proposed fix (skip rendering provider on editor route) is already implemented.

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 5 proposes calling `onOverlayChange` on every mouse move, but this triggers the full pipeline: applyTimelineEdit → materializeData → rowsToConfig → React state update → Remotion re-render. This could cause significant lag during drag, especially with complex timelines.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The data flow is: onOverlayChange → applyTimelineEdit (useTimelineData.ts:297) → materializeData → rowsToConfig (timeline-data.ts:227-256, iterates all clips) → setState → Remotion Player re-render. This is much heavier than the current dragOverride approach which only updates a single React state. The plan mentions throttling but doesn't specify a concrete implementation — requestAnimationFrame alone won't prevent the heavy materialization from running.

unresolved_flags.concern:
Step 9 render approach (MediaRecorder + canvas.captureStream) is speculative — Remotion Player does not render to a raw `` element; it uses a standard DOM tree with `
`, `
unresolved_flags.evidence: RemotionPreview.tsx passes `component={TimelineRenderer}` to Remotion's Player. TimelineRenderer (compositions/TimelineRenderer.tsx) renders standard React/Remotion components (AbsoluteFill, Sequence, etc.), not canvas. The Player renders these as DOM elements. The plan's assumption 'The Remotion Player exposes its canvas element for MediaRecorder capture' is false — there is no canvas to capture.
